questions.question: The diagram shows a simple d.c. motor. Which part is the split-ring commutator?n[Diagram showing magnets, coil, and four labelled parts A, B, C, D]
options: The split ring connected to the coil ends
The permanent magnets on the sides
The rectangular coil of wire
The carbon brushes touching the ring
questions.explanation:
Based on standard IGCSE diagrams (like Fig 18.56), the split-ring commutator is the circular ring divided into two halves that rotates with the coil.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A student tests a motor and records the following data:nnTrial | Current / A | Resultn--- | --- | ---n1 | 1.0 | Rotates slowlyn2 | 2.0 | Rotates fastern3 | 0.0 | StationarynnWhat can be concluded from this data?
options: The speed of rotation increases as the current increases
The motor requires a minimum of 2.0 A to function
The direction of rotation depends on the current magnitude
The current has no effect on the mechanical energy output
questions.explanation:
The data clearly shows that increasing the current from 1.0 A to 2.0 A results in a faster rotation speed, supporting the theory that current affects the magnitude of the force.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: In a simple d.c. motor, the direction of the current in the motor coil is reversed every half-revolution. Which part of the motor enables this?
options: Split-ring commutator
Brushes
Coil
Permanent magnets
questions.explanation:
This is a direct question from the review section. The split-ring commutator is specifically designed to reverse current every 180 degrees of rotation.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which row correctly identifies how to reverse the direction of the force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field?
options: Reverse the magnetic field OR reverse the current
Reverse the magnetic field AND reverse the current
Increase the current and keep field the same
Decrease the current and reverse the field
questions.explanation:
To reverse the direction of the force, only one factor (current or field) must be reversed. Reversing both would result in the same original force direction.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A d.c. motor is used in a toy drone. Why are permanent magnets used in the motor?
options: To provide a constant magnetic field for the coil to interact with
To ensure the motor always spins in an anticlockwise direction
To convert kinetic energy back into electrical energy for the battery
To act as a safety switch that breaks the circuit if it gets too hot
questions.explanation:
Permanent magnets provide the external magnetic field necessary for the current-carrying coil to experience a force (the motor effect).

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
